---
title: "RStudio Server on SPCS"
subtitle: "Native R IDE in a custom container service"
---

## Overview

**RStudio Server on SPCS** runs the open-source RStudio IDE as a **long-running
Snowpark Container Services (SPCS) service**. You get a full native R session —
Connections pane, debugger, Quarto, plots — **inside the Snowflake security
perimeter**, with **RSnowflake** and **snowflakeR** baked into the image.

This chapter is the **walkthrough**. Deep reference material lives in package
vignettes and the deploy kit under `snowflakeR/inst/rstudio-spcs/`.

::: {.callout-note}
**Prerequisites:** SPCS compute pool, image repository, role with `CREATE SERVICE`,
and (for in-account builds) Snowflake CLI ≥ 3.16 with Image Builder preview.
:::

## Learning objectives

- Decide when RStudio-on-SPCS fits vs Workspace, Posit Native App, or Remote Dev
- Provision stages, build an image, and deploy a service end-to-end
- Connect with RSnowflake and snowflakeR in a native R session
- Run smoke tests and recover the ingress URL after redeploy

---

## When to choose this path {#sec-when}

| Choose RStudio on SPCS | Choose another path |
|------------------------|---------------------|
| RStudio UX is required; you accept operating Docker + pool | Notebook-first → [Workspace](../05_workspaces_overview/index.qmd) |
| DIY image control (R version, packages, Conda) | Managed IDE → Posit Workbench Native App ([ch. 4](../04_rstudio_posit_vscode/index.qmd)) |
| No Posit Marketplace license yet | VS Code / Cursor → Snowflake Remote Dev |

Comparison matrix: [Workspaces overview — related paths](../05_workspaces_overview/index.qmd).

---

## Architecture {#sec-arch}

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  user[R user browser]
  ingress[SPCS public ingress]
  svc[RStudio Server container]
  token["/snowflake/session/token"]
  host[SNOWFLAKE_HOST internal gateway]
  wh[Snowflake warehouse]

  user --> ingress --> svc
  svc --> token
  svc --> host
  svc --> wh
```

| Component | Role |
|-----------|------|
| **RStudio Server** | Native R IDE on port 8787 |
| **RSnowflake** | `dbConnect(Snowflake())` via SQL REST API + SPCS OAuth |
| **snowflakeR** | ML APIs via reticulate → Snowpark Python |
| **Miniconda `snowflake_ml`** | Shared Python env for reticulate (not Ubuntu system Python) |
| **Stage volumes** | Persist workspace files and user R libraries |

---

## Deploy kit {#sec-kit}

The reference implementation ships with **snowflakeR**:

```r
system.file("rstudio-spcs", package = "snowflakeR")
```

Public source: [snowflakeR/inst/rstudio-spcs](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflakeR/tree/main/inst/rstudio-spcs).

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `README.md` | Kit entry point |
| `config.example.env` | Account, registry, pool, passwords |
| `provision.sh` | Render + run SQL templates from `config.env` |
| `provision.sql.template` | Stages + pool resume |
| `provision_image_builder_eai.sql.template` | Egress for in-account image build |
| `build_snowflake.sh` / `build_local.sh` | Image build paths |
| `deploy_service.sh` | `CREATE SERVICE` |
| `smoke_test.R` / `smoke_test.py` | Validation |

**Vignettes (detail):**

- [snowflakeR: rstudio-spcs](../../vignettes/snowflakeR/rstudio-spcs.html) — kit, reticulate, `sfr_connect_spcs()`
- [RSnowflake: spcs-custom-services](../../vignettes/RSnowflake/spcs-custom-services.html) — DBI auth, REST API limits

---

## End-to-end steps {#sec-steps}

### 1. Configure

```bash
cd inst/rstudio-spcs
cp config.example.env config.env
# Edit: SNOW_CONNECTION, account, registry URL, pool, roles, RSTUDIO_PASSWORD
```

All object names live in `config.env` — the same file used by build and deploy scripts.

### 2. Provision Snowflake objects

```bash
./provision.sh
./provision.sh --eai    # once, if using SPCS Image Builder (SNOW_EAI_ROLE, default ACCOUNTADMIN)
```

You need:

- **Image repository** with **OWNERSHIP** for your deploy role
- **Compute pool** (build + runtime can share a pool for pilots)
- **Stage** `VOLUMES` with subpaths `rstudio_workspace` and `rstudio_r_libs`

### 3. Build the image

**Preferred — in-account (Image Builder):**

```bash
source config.env
./build_snowflake.sh
```

Requires CLI ≥ 3.16 and `enable_spcs_build_image = true` in
`~/.snowflake/config.toml`.

**Fallback — local Docker:**

```bash
source config.env
PUSH=1 ./build_local.sh    # always linux/amd64 for SPCS
```

The build context uploads **pre-built snowflakeR/RSnowflake tarballs** when
possible — compiling from source inside the builder is slow. See
[R package tarball workflow](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflakeR#installation).

### 4. Deploy the service

```bash
source config.env
./deploy_service.sh
```

`service-spec.template.yaml` sets container env vars including
`SNOWFLAKE_WAREHOUSE` — required because RSnowflake cannot run `USE WAREHOUSE`
on the SQL REST API.

### 5. Open RStudio and smoke test

```sql
SHOW ENDPOINTS IN SERVICE MY_DB.MY_SCHEMA.RSTUDIO_SVC;
```

::: {.callout-warning}
## Ingress URL changes on redeploy

Each `DROP SERVICE` + `CREATE SERVICE` cycle assigns a **new** public hostname.
Re-run `SHOW ENDPOINTS` after every redeploy.
:::

1. Browser → ingress URL → Snowflake SSO
2. RStudio login: user `rstudio`, password from `RSTUDIO_PASSWORD` in `config.env`
3. In R Console: `source("~/smoke_test.R")`
4. In Terminal: `python3 ~/smoke_test.py`

---

## Using RSnowflake {#sec-rsnowflake}

```r
library(DBI)
library(RSnowflake)

con <- dbConnect(Snowflake())
dbGetQuery(con, "SELECT CURRENT_USER(), CURRENT_WAREHOUSE()")
```

Warehouse, database, schema, and role come from **service env vars** set at
deploy time. Full detail:
[vignette: spcs-custom-services](../../vignettes/RSnowflake/spcs-custom-services.html).

---

## Using snowflakeR {#sec-snowflaker}

**`sfr_connect()`** auto-detects **Workspace Notebooks** only. In this custom
SPCS service, use **`sfr_connect_spcs()`** from the kit:

```r
source("~/spcs_helpers.R")
conn <- sfr_connect_spcs()
```

The image configures **reticulate** with Miniconda Python at
`/opt/conda/envs/snowflake_ml/bin/python`. See the
[rstudio-spcs vignette](../../vignettes/snowflakeR/rstudio-spcs.html) for
`collect()` vs `to_pandas()` and OpenSSL notes.

---

## Operations {#sec-ops}

| Topic | Guidance |
|-------|----------|
| **Image updates** | Rebuild, push, redeploy; suspend/resume pool to force fresh pull |
| **Persistence** | Use stage volume mounts — not container ephemeral storage |
| **EAI** | Required for image **build** egress (Docker Hub, CRAN/PPM, PyPI); not for routine Snowflake queries via internal gateway |
| **Security** | Use ingress OAuth; set a strong `PASSWORD`; do not use `DISABLE_AUTH` |
| **Cost** | Dedicated service + pool — size `MIN_NODES` / `MAX_NODES` for pilots |

Stage file I/O: mount stages as volumes — RSnowflake does not support SQL
`GET`/`PUT`. Same pattern as [Parallel doSnowflake](../22_parallel_dosnowflake/index.qmd) workers.

---

## Related chapters {#sec-related}

- [RStudio, Posit & VS Code](../04_rstudio_posit_vscode/index.qmd) — desktop IDE and Posit Native App
- [CRE & ML Jobs](../09_custom_runtime_and_ml_jobs/index.qmd) — SPCS images for Workspace (different use case)
- [RSnowflake in Workspace](../14_rsnowflake_workspace/index.qmd) — `%%R` bootstrap path
- [Parallel doSnowflake](../22_parallel_dosnowflake/index.qmd) — batch R workers on SPCS
